action.reasoning: Observation: The 8-model uniform ensemble achieved 76.73% accuracy (new best). We have ~18.7 minutes left and 17 iterations remaining—time is the binding constraint. Analysis: Uniform weighting is likely suboptimal. Because we have train_cleaned.pt (~45k samples with partially corrected labels), we can quickly learn ensemble weights by optimizing a softmax-weighted average of the 8 models' logits against those cleaner labels. With only 8 weights and 45k samples, overfitting is negligible, and the optimization takes seconds after extracting logits. This should improve over uniform weighting at virtually zero cost. Decision: 1) Write learn_weights.py to extract single-crop logits from all 8 checkpoints on train_cleaned.pt and learn weights via minimizing cross-entropy (Adam, 300 steps). 2) Update predict.py to load learned weights from ensemble_weights.json if present, else fall back to uniform. 3) Run learning + local predict sanity check in one chained command. 4) Submit evaluation.
